questions.text: What is the largest source of river pollution in many cities?
questions.explanation: In many developing regions, untreated sewage from homes and cities is the largest contributor to river pollution.

questions.text: How does agriculture contribute to river pollution?
options: Rain washes fertilizers and pesticides into rivers
Farm animals drink too much water
questions.explanation: Fertilizers and pesticides used in farming can wash into nearby rivers during rainfall, polluting the water.

questions.text: Why can industrial waste be especially harmful to rivers?
questions.explanation: Industrial waste can contain heavy metals and toxic chemicals that damage ecosystems and make water unsafe.

questions.text: Once pollution enters a river, what usually happens next?
options: It spreads downstream through the river system
It stays only where it entered
It turns into clean water automatically
questions.explanation: River currents carry pollutants downstream, allowing contamination to spread far from the original source.

questions.text: Why can't rivers always clean themselves naturally?
options: Natural purification systems can be overwhelmed by large amounts of waste
questions.explanation: Rivers have natural cleaning processes, but excessive pollution can overwhelm these systems.

resultLabels.title: Beginner
resultLabels.message: You're just starting to explore how river pollution happens. The upcoming articles will reveal the main pollution sources and how they affect rivers.

resultLabels.title: Developing Understanding
resultLabels.message: You understand some of the main pollution sources. The next articles will help you see how these pollutants spread through river ecosystems.

resultLabels.title: Strong Understanding
resultLabels.message: You already understand many causes of river pollution. Now you're ready to explore the deeper science behind ecosystem impacts and restoration.
